WITH RECURSIVE
view_deps AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        dependent_view.relname AS view_name,
        source_table.relname AS depends_on,
        CASE WHEN source_table.relkind = $1 THEN $2 ELSE $3 END AS depends_on_matview
    FROM pg_depend
    JOIN pg_rewrite ON pg_depend.objid = pg_rewrite.oid
    JOIN pg_class AS dependent_view ON pg_rewrite.ev_class = dependent_view.oid
    JOIN pg_class AS source_table ON pg_depend.refobjid = source_table.oid
    JOIN pg_namespace dependent_ns ON dependent_ns.oid = dependent_view.relnamespace
    JOIN pg_namespace source_ns ON source_ns.oid = source_table.relnamespace
    WHERE dependent_view.relkind = $4
      AND source_table.relkind IN ($5, $6, $7)
      AND pg_depend.deptype = $8
      AND dependent_ns.nspname = $9
      AND source_ns.nspname = $10
),
mv_dependencies AS (
    SELECT view_name, depends_on FROM view_deps WHERE depends_on_matview = $11
),
all_mvs AS (
    SELECT schemaname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $12
),
dependency_depth AS (
    SELECT am.schemaname, am.matviewname AS view_name, $13 AS depth, ARRAY[am.matviewname] AS path
    FROM all_mvs am
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT $14 FROM mv_dependencies md WHERE md.view_name = am.matviewname)
    UNION ALL
    SELECT am.schemaname, md.view_name, dd.depth + $15, dd.path || md.view_name
    FROM mv_dependencies md
    JOIN dependency_depth dd ON md.depends_on = dd.view_name
    JOIN all_mvs am ON am.matviewname = md.view_name
    WHERE NOT (md.view_name = ANY(dd.path))
),
max_depths AS (
    SELECT schemaname, view_name, MAX(depth) as max_depth
    FROM dependency_depth
    GROUP BY schemaname, view_name
)
SELECT format($16, schemaname, view_name) AS refresh_stmt
FROM max_depths
ORDER BY max_depth, schemaname, view_name

DO $$
DECLARE
    dup_count BIGINT;
BEGIN
    -- Check for duplicate person_ids in view_riksdagen_politician
    BEGIN
        WITH duplicate_politicians AS (
            SELECT person_id, COUNT(*) as count
            FROM view_riksdagen_politician
            GROUP BY person_id
            HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
        )
        SELECT COUNT(*) INTO dup_count FROM duplicate_politicians;
        
        INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
            'Data Quality',
            'Duplicate Check: view_riksdagen_politician.person_id',
            CASE WHEN dup_count > 0 THEN 'FAIL' ELSE 'PASS' END,
            CASE WHEN dup_count > 0 THEN 3 ELSE 1 END,
            'Found ' || dup_count || ' duplicate person_ids',
            CASE WHEN dup_count > 0 THEN 'Investigate and remove duplicates in view definition' ELSE NULL END
        );
    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
        INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
            'Data Quality',
            'Duplicate Check: view_riksdagen_politician',
            'INFO',
            1,
            'View does not exist or cannot be checked: ' || SQLERRM,
            NULL
        );
    END;
END $$

SELECT 
    temporal_granularity AS "Granularity",
    COUNT(*) AS "View Count",
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $1 THEN $2
        WHEN $3 THEN $4
        WHEN $5 THEN $6
        WHEN $7 THEN $8
        WHEN $9 THEN $10
        ELSE $11
    END AS "Sampling Strategy"
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $12::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $13
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $14::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $15
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
) view_classification
GROUP BY temporal_granularity
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $16 THEN $17
        WHEN $18 THEN $19
        WHEN $20 THEN $21
        WHEN $22 THEN $23
        WHEN $24 THEN $25
        ELSE $26
    END

            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $1::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $2
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $3::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $4
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
),
temporal_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        temporal_granularity,
        COUNT(*) AS view_count,
        CASE temporal_granularity
            WHEN $5 THEN $6
            WHEN $7 THEN $8
            WHEN $9 THEN $10
            WHEN $11 THEN $12
            WHEN $13 THEN $14
            ELSE $15
        END AS sampling_strategy
    FROM view_temporal_classification
    GROUP BY temporal_granularity
),
overall_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        $16 AS category,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = $17 AND tablename NOT LIKE $18 AND tablename NOT LIKE $19) AS total_tables,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $20) AS total_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $21) AS total_materialized_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $22) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $23) AS total_views_all,
        $24::int AS default_sample_size,
        $25::int AS party_sample_size,
        $26::int AS committee_sample_size,
        $27::int AS person_sample_size,
        $28::int AS document_sample_size,
        $29::int AS voting_sample_size,
        $30::int AS worldbank_sample_size,
        $31::int AS complete_threshold
)
SELECT 
    $32 AS metric,
    temporal_granularity AS granularity,
    view_count::bigint AS count,
    sampling_strategy AS strategy
FROM temporal_summary
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $33 AS metric,
    $34 AS granularity,
    (total_tables + total_views_all)::bigint AS count,
    $35 || total_tables || $36 || total_views || $37 || total_materialized_views AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $38 AS metric,
    $39 AS granularity,
    default_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $40 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $41 AS metric,
    $42 AS granularity,
    party_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $43 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $44 AS metric,
    $45 AS granularity,
    document_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $46 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $47 AS metric,
    $48 AS granularity,
    voting_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $49 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $50 AS metric,
    $51 AS granularity,
    worldbank_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $52 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $53 AS metric,
    $54 AS granularity,
    complete_threshold::bigint AS count,
    $55 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
ORDER BY metric, granularity

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_extraction_stats AS
WITH view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $1::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $2
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $3::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $4
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
),
temporal_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        temporal_granularity,
        COUNT(*) AS view_count,
        CASE temporal_granularity
            WHEN $5 THEN $6
            WHEN $7 THEN $8
            WHEN $9 THEN $10
            WHEN $11 THEN $12
            WHEN $13 THEN $14
            ELSE $15
        END AS sampling_strategy
    FROM view_temporal_classification
    GROUP BY temporal_granularity
),
overall_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        $16 AS category,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = $17 AND tablename NOT LIKE $18 AND tablename NOT LIKE $19) AS total_tables,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $20) AS total_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $21) AS total_materialized_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $22) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $23) AS total_views_all,
        $24::int AS default_sample_size,
        $25::int AS party_sample_size,
        $26::int AS committee_sample_size,
        $27::int AS person_sample_size,
        $28::int AS document_sample_size,
        $29::int AS voting_sample_size,
        $30::int AS worldbank_sample_size,
        $31::int AS complete_threshold
)
SELECT 
    $32 AS metric,
    temporal_granularity AS granularity,
    view_count::bigint AS count,
    sampling_strategy AS strategy
FROM temporal_summary
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $33 AS metric,
    $34 AS granularity,
    (total_tables + total_views_all)::bigint AS count,
    $35 || total_tables || $36 || total_views || $37 || total_materialized_views AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $38 AS metric,
    $39 AS granularity,
    default_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $40 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $41 AS metric,
    $42 AS granularity,
    party_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $43 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $44 AS metric,
    $45 AS granularity,
    document_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $46 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $47 AS metric,
    $48 AS granularity,
    voting_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $49 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $50 AS metric,
    $51 AS granularity,
    worldbank_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $52 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $53 AS metric,
    $54 AS granularity,
    complete_threshold::bigint AS count,
    $55 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
ORDER BY metric, granularity

COPY (
WITH view_rels AS (
  SELECT
    c.oid     AS view_oid,
    n.nspname AS view_schema,
    c.relname AS view_name
  FROM pg_class c
  JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
  WHERE c.relkind IN ('v','m')
    AND n.nspname NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema','public')
)
SELECT DISTINCT
  vr.view_schema,
  vr.view_name,
  rn.nspname AS dep_schema,
  rc.relname AS dep_name
FROM view_rels vr
JOIN pg_rewrite rw
  ON rw.ev_class = vr.view_oid
 AND rw.rulename = '_RETURN'
JOIN pg_depend d
  ON d.classid = 'pg_rewrite'::regclass
 AND d.objid   = rw.oid
 AND d.refclassid = 'pg_class'::regclass
 AND d.deptype IN ('n','a')
JOIN pg_class rc ON rc.oid = d.refobjid
JOIN pg_namespace rn ON rn.oid = rc.relnamespace
WHERE rn.nspname NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema','public')
ORDER BY 1,2,3,4
) TO STDOUT WITH (FORMAT csv, HEADER true)

DO $$
DECLARE
    v_stddev NUMERIC;
    v_avg NUMERIC;
    v_coefficient NUMERIC;
BEGIN
    BEGIN
        WITH person_party_distribution AS (
            SELECT party, COUNT(*) as count
            FROM person_data
            WHERE party IS NOT NULL
            GROUP BY party
        )
        SELECT 
            STDDEV(count),
            AVG(count),
            CASE WHEN AVG(count) > 0 THEN STDDEV(count) / AVG(count) ELSE 0 END
        INTO v_stddev, v_avg, v_coefficient
        FROM person_party_distribution;
        
        INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
            'Data Quality',
            'Party Distribution Balance',
            CASE 
                WHEN v_coefficient > 1.5 THEN 'WARN'
                ELSE 'PASS'
            END,
            CASE 
                WHEN v_coefficient > 1.5 THEN 2
                ELSE 1
            END,
            'Distribution coefficient: ' || COALESCE(ROUND(v_coefficient, 2)::TEXT, 'N/A') || 
            ' | Avg per party: ' || COALESCE(ROUND(v_avg, 0)::TEXT, 'N/A'),
            CASE 
                WHEN v_coefficient > 1.5 THEN 'Party distribution imbalance detected - investigate if intentional'
                ELSE NULL
            END
        );
    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
        INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
            'Data Quality',
            'Party Distribution Balance',
            'INFO',
            1,
            'Could not analyze: ' || SQLERRM,
            NULL
        );
    END;
END $$

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_extraction_stats AS
WITH view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $1::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $2
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $3::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $4
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
),
temporal_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        temporal_granularity,
        COUNT(*) AS view_count,
        CASE temporal_granularity
            WHEN $5 THEN $6
            WHEN $7 THEN $8
            WHEN $9 THEN $10
            WHEN $11 THEN $12
            WHEN $13 THEN $14
            ELSE $15
        END AS sampling_strategy
    FROM view_temporal_classification
    GROUP BY temporal_granularity
),
overall_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        $16 AS category,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = $17 AND tablename NOT LIKE $18 AND tablename NOT LIKE $19) AS total_tables,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $20) AS total_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $21) AS total_materialized_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $22) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $23) AS total_views_all,
        $24::int AS default_sample_size,
        $25::int AS party_sample_size,
        $26::int AS committee_sample_size,
        $27::int AS person_sample_size,
        $28::int AS document_sample_size,
        $29::int AS voting_sample_size,
        $30::int AS worldbank_sample_size,
        $31::int AS complete_threshold
)
SELECT 
    $32 AS metric,
    temporal_granularity AS granularity,
    view_count::bigint AS count,
    sampling_strategy AS strategy
FROM temporal_summary
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $33 AS metric,
    $34 AS granularity,
    (total_tables + total_views_all)::bigint AS count,
    $35 || total_tables || $36 || total_views || $37 || total_materialized_views AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $38 AS metric,
    $39 AS granularity,
    default_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $40 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $41 AS metric,
    $42 AS granularity,
    party_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $43 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $44 AS metric,
    $45 AS granularity,
    document_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $46 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $47 AS metric,
    $48 AS granularity,
    voting_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $49 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $50 AS metric,
    $51 AS granularity,
    worldbank_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $52 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $53 AS metric,
    $54 AS granularity,
    complete_threshold::bigint AS count,
    $55 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
ORDER BY metric, granularity

WITH RECURSIVE 
        -- Get all materialized view dependencies
        view_deps AS (
            SELECT DISTINCT
                dependent_view.relname AS view_name,
                source_table.relname AS depends_on,
                CASE 
                    WHEN source_table.relkind = $1 THEN $2
                    ELSE $3
                END AS depends_on_matview
            FROM pg_depend
            JOIN pg_rewrite ON pg_depend.objid = pg_rewrite.oid
            JOIN pg_class AS dependent_view ON pg_rewrite.ev_class = dependent_view.oid
            JOIN pg_class AS source_table ON pg_depend.refobjid = source_table.oid
            JOIN pg_namespace dependent_ns ON dependent_ns.oid = dependent_view.relnamespace
            JOIN pg_namespace source_ns ON source_ns.oid = source_table.relnamespace
            WHERE dependent_view.relkind = $4
              AND source_table.relkind IN ($5, $6, $7)
              AND pg_depend.deptype = $8
              AND dependent_ns.nspname = $9
              AND source_ns.nspname = $10
        ),
        -- Build dependency graph (only MV dependencies)
        mv_dependencies AS (
            SELECT view_name, depends_on
            FROM view_deps
            WHERE depends_on_matview = $11
        ),
        -- Get all materialized views
        all_mvs AS (
            SELECT matviewname AS view_name
            FROM pg_matviews
            WHERE schemaname = $12
        ),
        -- Calculate dependency depth using recursive CTE
        dependency_depth AS (
            -- Base case: views with no MV dependencies
            SELECT 
                am.view_name,
                $13 AS depth,
                ARRAY[am.view_name] AS path
            FROM all_mvs am
            WHERE NOT EXISTS (
                SELECT $14 FROM mv_dependencies md 
                WHERE md.view_name = am.view_name
            )
            
            UNION ALL
            
            -- Recursive case: views that depend on views we've already processed
            SELECT 
                md.view_name,
                dd.depth + $15,
                dd.path || md.view_name
            FROM mv_dependencies md
            JOIN dependency_depth dd ON md.depends_on = dd.view_name
            WHERE NOT (md.view_name = ANY(dd.path)) -- Prevent cycles
        ),
        -- Get maximum depth for each view
        max_depths AS (
            SELECT 
                view_name,
                MAX(depth) as max_depth
            FROM dependency_depth
            GROUP BY view_name
        )
        -- Output in refresh order
        SELECT 
            $16 AS schemaname,
            view_name AS matviewname,
            max_depth AS dependency_level
        FROM max_depths
        ORDER BY max_depth, view_name

WITH view_counts AS (
    SELECT schemaname,
           viewname,
           row_count
    FROM cia_view_row_counts
    WHERE viewname != $1
      AND viewname != $2
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL VIEW CLASSIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
-- Identify views by temporal granularity based on naming patterns
-- This enables stratified sampling across time periods
-- Uses reusable cia_classify_temporal_view() function
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        vc.schemaname,
        vc.viewname,
        vc.row_count,
        c.temporal_granularity,
        c.samples_per_bucket
    FROM view_counts vc
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(vc.viewname) AS c
    WHERE vc.row_count > $3
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL COLUMN DETECTION
-- ============================================================================
-- Automatically detect temporal columns in each view
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_columns AS (
    SELECT 
        c.table_schema AS schemaname,
        c.table_name AS viewname,
        c.column_name AS temporal_column,
        c.data_type,
        -- Prioritize common temporal column names
        CASE 
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($4, $5) THEN $6
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($7, $8, $9) THEN $10
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($11, $12, $13) THEN $14
            WHEN c.column_name LIKE $15 THEN $16
            ELSE $17
        END AS priority
    FROM information_schema.columns c
    WHERE c.table_schema = $18
      AND c.data_type IN ($19, $20, $21)
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($22, $23) -- Exclude technical columns
),
view_primary_temporal_column AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (schemaname, viewname)
        schemaname,
        viewname,
        temporal_column
    FROM view_temporal_columns
    ORDER BY schemaname, viewname, priority
),
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE STRATIFIED SAMPLING QUERIES
-- ============================================================================
view_extract AS (
    SELECT 
        vtc.schemaname,
        vtc.viewname,
        vtc.row_count,
        vtc.temporal_granularity,
        vtc.samples_per_bucket,
        vptc.temporal_column,
        -- Smart sample size calculation:
        -- 1. Complete extraction for small views (< threshold)
        -- 2. Extended samples for analytical trend views (election proximity, seasonal activity, career trajectory)
        -- 3. Extended samples for party/committee/person views
        -- 4. Extended samples for document/voting/worldbank views
        -- 5. Default sample size for others
        CASE 
            -- Complete extraction for small views
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $24::int THEN vtc.row_count
            -- Extended samples for analytical trend views (500 rows for better temporal coverage)
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($25,
                                   $26,
                                   $27) THEN
                LEAST($28::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for party-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $29 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $30 THEN 
                LEAST($31::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for committee-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $32 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $33 THEN 
                LEAST($34::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for person/politician views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $35 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $36 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $37 THEN 
                LEAST($38::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for document-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $39 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $40 THEN 
                LEAST($41::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for voting-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $42 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $43 THEN 
                LEAST($44::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for worldbank data
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $45 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $46 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $47 THEN 
                LEAST($48::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Default sample size
            ELSE LEAST($49::int, vtc.row_count)
        END AS sample_rows,
        -- Extraction type for logging
        CASE 
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $50::int THEN $51
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($52,
                                   $53,
                                   $54) THEN $55
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $56 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $57 THEN $58
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $59 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $60 THEN $61
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $62 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $63 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $64 THEN $65
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $66 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $67 THEN $68
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $69 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $70 THEN $71
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $72 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $73 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $74 THEN $75
            ELSE $76
        END AS extraction_type,
        CASE WHEN vtc.viewname LIKE $77 THEN vtc.viewname ELSE $78 || vtc.viewname END AS file_prefix
    FROM view_temporal_classification vtc
    LEFT JOIN view_primary_temporal_column vptc 
        ON vtc.schemaname = vptc.schemaname 
        AND vtc.viewname = vptc.viewname
)
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE EXTRACTION COMMANDS WITH TEMPORAL STRATIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
SELECT 
    CASE 
        -- =====================================================================
        -- DAILY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by day
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $79 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $80 || $81 ||
                $82 ||
                $83 ||
                $84 ||
                $85 ||
                $86 ||
                $87 ||
                $88 ||
                $89 ||
                $90 || $91 ||
                $92 || $93,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- WEEKLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by week
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $94 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $95 || $96 ||
                $97 ||
                $98 ||
                $99 ||
                $100 ||
                $101 ||
                $102 ||
                $103 ||
                $104 ||
                $105 || $106 ||
                $107 || $108,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- MONTHLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by month
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $109 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $110 || $111 ||
                $112 ||
                $113 ||
                $114 ||
                $115 ||
                $116 ||
                $117 ||
                $118 ||
                $119 ||
                $120 || $121 ||
                $122 || $123,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- ANNUAL VIEWS: Stratified sampling by year
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $124 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $125 || $126 ||
                $127 ||
                $128 ||
                $129 ||
                $130 ||
                $131 ||
                $132 ||
                $133 ||
                $134 || $135 ||
                $136 || $137,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- TEMPORAL TREND VIEWS: Sample across all time periods
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $138 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $139 || $140 ||
                $141 ||
                $142 ||
                $143 ||
                $144 ||
                $145 ||
                $146 ||
                $147 ||
                $148 ||
                $149 || $150 ||
                $151 || $152,
                viewname,
                temporal_column, temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- NON-TEMPORAL VIEWS: Use random sampling with smart sample sizes
        -- =====================================================================
        ELSE
            format(
                $153 || $154 ||
                $155 || $156 ||
                $157 || $158,
                extraction_type,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                row_count,
                schemaname,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                file_prefix
            )
    END AS extraction_command
FROM view_extract
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $159 THEN $160
        WHEN $161 THEN $162
        WHEN $163 THEN $164
        WHEN $165 THEN $166
        WHEN $167 THEN $168
        ELSE $169
    END,
    viewname

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_view_levels AS
WITH RECURSIVE 
all_views AS (
    SELECT viewname AS view_name, $1 AS view_type
    FROM pg_views 
    WHERE schemaname = $2
      AND viewname NOT LIKE $3
    UNION ALL
    SELECT matviewname AS view_name, $4 AS view_type
    FROM pg_matviews 
    WHERE schemaname = $5
      AND matviewname NOT LIKE $6
),
view_only_deps AS (
    SELECT vd.view_name, vd.depends_on
    FROM tmp_view_dependencies vd
    WHERE vd.dependency_type IN ($7, $8)
),
dependency_depth AS (
    -- Base case: views with no view dependencies (only table dependencies)
    SELECT 
        av.view_name,
        av.view_type,
        $9 AS depth,
        ARRAY[av.view_name] AS path
    FROM all_views av
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT $10 FROM view_only_deps vod 
        WHERE vod.view_name = av.view_name
    )
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: views depending on already processed views
    SELECT 
        vod.view_name,
        av.view_type,
        dd.depth + $11,
        dd.path || vod.view_name
    FROM view_only_deps vod
    JOIN dependency_depth dd ON vod.depends_on = dd.view_name
    JOIN all_views av ON vod.view_name = av.view_name
    WHERE NOT (vod.view_name = ANY(dd.path)) -- Prevent cycles
)
SELECT 
    view_name,
    view_type,
    MAX(depth) as dependency_level,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(depth) BETWEEN $12 AND $13 THEN $14
        WHEN MAX(depth) = $15 THEN $16
        WHEN MAX(depth) = $17 THEN $18
        WHEN MAX(depth) >= $19 THEN $20
    END AS complexity_category
FROM dependency_depth
GROUP BY view_name, view_type

DO $$
DECLARE
    v_table TEXT;
    v_count BIGINT;
    v_critical_tables TEXT[] := ARRAY[
        'person_data',
        'assignment_data',
        'ballot_data',
        'vote_data',
        'document_data',
        'sweden_political_party',
        'committee_proposal_data',
        'document_element',
        'document_person_reference_data'
    ];
BEGIN
    FOREACH v_table IN ARRAY v_critical_tables
    LOOP
        BEGIN
            EXECUTE format('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM %I', v_table) INTO v_count;
            
            IF v_count = 0 THEN
                INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
                    'Data Quality',
                    'Critical Table: ' || v_table,
                    'FAIL',
                    4,
                    'Table is empty (0 rows)',
                    'Load data into table or check data import processes. See TROUBLESHOOTING_EMPTY_VIEWS.md'
                );
            ELSIF v_count < 100 THEN
                INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
                    'Data Quality',
                    'Critical Table: ' || v_table,
                    'WARN',
                    2,
                    'Table has only ' || v_count || ' rows',
                    'Verify data completeness and import status'
                );
            ELSE
                INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
                    'Data Quality',
                    'Critical Table: ' || v_table,
                    'PASS',
                    1,
                    'Table has ' || v_count || ' rows',
                    NULL
                );
            END IF;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
                'Data Quality',
                'Critical Table: ' || v_table,
                'INFO',
                1,
                'Table does not exist or cannot be accessed: ' || SQLERRM,
                'Verify table name and permissions'
            );
        END;
    END LOOP;
END $$

INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
        $5,
        $6,
        CASE WHEN max_depth > $7 THEN $8 ELSE $9 END,
        CASE WHEN max_depth > $10 THEN $11 ELSE $12 END,
        $13 || COALESCE(max_depth::TEXT, $14) || 
        $15 || COALESCE(deep_views::TEXT, $16) ||
        $17 || COALESCE(view_count::TEXT, $18),
        CASE WHEN max_depth > $19 
            THEN $20
            ELSE $21
        END
    )

COPY (
WITH allowed_schemas AS (
  -- ✅ put the schemas you WANT here
  SELECT unnest(ARRAY['cia','riksdagen']) AS nspname
),
view_rels AS (
  SELECT
    c.oid     AS view_oid,
    n.nspname AS view_schema,
    c.relname AS view_name,
    c.relkind AS view_kind
  FROM pg_class c
  JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
  JOIN allowed_schemas a ON a.nspname = n.nspname
  WHERE c.relkind IN ('v','m')  -- view + matview
)
SELECT DISTINCT
  vr.view_schema,
  vr.view_name,
  CASE vr.view_kind WHEN 'v' THEN 'view' WHEN 'm' THEN 'materialized_view' END AS view_type,

  rn.nspname AS referenced_schema,
  rc.relname AS referenced_name,
  CASE rc.relkind
    WHEN 'r' THEN 'table'
    WHEN 'p' THEN 'partitioned_table'
    WHEN 'v' THEN 'view'
    WHEN 'm' THEN 'materialized_view'
    WHEN 'f' THEN 'foreign_table'
    WHEN 'S' THEN 'sequence'
    ELSE rc.relkind::text
  END AS referenced_type
FROM view_rels vr
JOIN pg_rewrite rw
  ON rw.ev_class = vr.view_oid
 AND rw.rulename = '_RETURN'
JOIN pg_depend d
  ON d.classid = 'pg_rewrite'::regclass
 AND d.objid   = rw.oid
 AND d.refclassid = 'pg_class'::regclass
 AND d.deptype IN ('n','a')
JOIN pg_class rc ON rc.oid = d.refobjid
JOIN pg_namespace rn ON rn.oid = rc.relnamespace
-- ✅ only keep referenced objects in allowed schemas too (remove if you *want* cross-schema refs)
JOIN allowed_schemas ar ON ar.nspname = rn.nspname
ORDER BY 1,2,4,5
) TO STDOUT WITH (FORMAT csv, HEADER true)

SELECT 
    temporal_granularity AS "Granularity",
    COUNT(*) AS "View Count",
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $1 THEN $2
        WHEN $3 THEN $4
        WHEN $5 THEN $6
        WHEN $7 THEN $8
        WHEN $9 THEN $10
        ELSE $11
    END AS "Sampling Strategy"
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $12::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $13
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $14::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $15
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
) view_classification
GROUP BY temporal_granularity
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $16 THEN $17
        WHEN $18 THEN $19
        WHEN $20 THEN $21
        WHEN $22 THEN $23
        WHEN $24 THEN $25
        ELSE $26
    END

WITH view_counts AS (
    SELECT schemaname,
           viewname,
           row_count
    FROM cia_view_row_counts
    WHERE viewname != $1
      AND viewname != $2
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL VIEW CLASSIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
-- Identify views by temporal granularity based on naming patterns
-- This enables stratified sampling across time periods
-- Uses reusable cia_classify_temporal_view() function
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        vc.schemaname,
        vc.viewname,
        vc.row_count,
        c.temporal_granularity,
        c.samples_per_bucket
    FROM view_counts vc
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(vc.viewname) AS c
    -- CHANGED: Include ALL views, even empty ones (for header-only CSV files)
    -- WHERE vc.row_count > 0  -- REMOVED: No longer skip empty views
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL COLUMN DETECTION
-- ============================================================================
-- Automatically detect temporal columns in each view
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_columns AS (
    SELECT 
        c.table_schema AS schemaname,
        c.table_name AS viewname,
        c.column_name AS temporal_column,
        c.data_type,
        -- Prioritize common temporal column names
        CASE 
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($3, $4) THEN $5
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($6, $7, $8) THEN $9
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($10, $11, $12) THEN $13
            WHEN c.column_name LIKE $14 THEN $15
            ELSE $16
        END AS priority
    FROM information_schema.columns c
    WHERE c.table_schema = $17
      AND c.data_type IN ($18, $19, $20)
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($21, $22) -- Exclude technical columns
),
view_primary_temporal_column AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (schemaname, viewname)
        schemaname,
        viewname,
        temporal_column
    FROM view_temporal_columns
    ORDER BY schemaname, viewname, priority
),
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE STRATIFIED SAMPLING QUERIES
-- ============================================================================
view_extract AS (
    SELECT 
        vtc.schemaname,
        vtc.viewname,
        vtc.row_count,
        vtc.temporal_granularity,
        vtc.samples_per_bucket,
        vptc.temporal_column,
        -- Smart sample size calculation:
        -- 1. Complete extraction for small views (< threshold)
        -- 2. Extended samples for analytical trend views (election proximity, seasonal activity, career trajectory)
        -- 3. Extended samples for party/committee/person views
        -- 4. Extended samples for document/voting/worldbank views
        -- 5. Default sample size for others
        CASE 
            -- Complete extraction for small views
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $23::int THEN vtc.row_count
            -- Extended samples for analytical trend views (500 rows for better temporal coverage)
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($24,
                                   $25,
                                   $26) THEN
                LEAST($27::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for party-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $28 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $29 THEN 
                LEAST($30::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for committee-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $31 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $32 THEN 
                LEAST($33::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for person/politician views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $34 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $35 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $36 THEN 
                LEAST($37::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for document-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $38 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $39 THEN 
                LEAST($40::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for voting-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $41 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $42 THEN 
                LEAST($43::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for worldbank data
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $44 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $45 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $46 THEN 
                LEAST($47::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Default sample size
            ELSE LEAST($48::int, vtc.row_count)
        END AS sample_rows,
        -- Extraction type for logging
        CASE 
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $49::int THEN $50
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($51,
                                   $52,
                                   $53) THEN $54
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $55 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $56 THEN $57
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $58 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $59 THEN $60
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $61 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $62 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $63 THEN $64
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $65 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $66 THEN $67
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $68 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $69 THEN $70
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $71 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $72 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $73 THEN $74
            ELSE $75
        END AS extraction_type,
        CASE WHEN vtc.viewname LIKE $76 THEN vtc.viewname ELSE $77 || vtc.viewname END AS file_prefix
    FROM view_temporal_classification vtc
    LEFT JOIN view_primary_temporal_column vptc 
        ON vtc.schemaname = vptc.schemaname 
        AND vtc.viewname = vptc.viewname
)
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE EXTRACTION COMMANDS WITH TEMPORAL STRATIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
SELECT 
    CASE 
        -- =====================================================================
        -- DAILY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by day
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $78 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $79 || $80 ||
                $81 ||
                $82 ||
                $83 ||
                $84 ||
                $85 ||
                $86 ||
                $87 ||
                $88 ||
                $89 || $90 ||
                $91 || $92 || $93 || $94 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $95 THEN $96 || $97
                    ELSE $98 || $99
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- WEEKLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by week
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $100 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $101 || $102 ||
                $103 ||
                $104 ||
                $105 ||
                $106 ||
                $107 ||
                $108 ||
                $109 ||
                $110 ||
                $111 || $112 ||
                $113 || $114 || $115 || $116 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $117 THEN $118 || $119
                    ELSE $120 || $121
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- MONTHLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by month
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $122 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $123 || $124 ||
                $125 ||
                $126 ||
                $127 ||
                $128 ||
                $129 ||
                $130 ||
                $131 ||
                $132 ||
                $133 || $134 ||
                $135 || $136 || $137 || $138 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $139 THEN $140 || $141
                    ELSE $142 || $143
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- ANNUAL VIEWS: Stratified sampling by year
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $144 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $145 || $146 ||
                $147 ||
                $148 ||
                $149 ||
                $150 ||
                $151 ||
                $152 ||
                $153 ||
                $154 || $155 ||
                $156 || $157 || $158 || $159 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $160 THEN $161 || $162
                    ELSE $163 || $164
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- TEMPORAL TREND VIEWS: Sample across all time periods
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $165 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $166 || $167 ||
                $168 ||
                $169 ||
                $170 ||
                $171 ||
                $172 ||
                $173 ||
                $174 ||
                $175 ||
                $176 || $177 ||
                $178 || $179 || $180 || $181 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $182 THEN $183 || $184
                    ELSE $185 || $186
                END,
                viewname,
                temporal_column, temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- NON-TEMPORAL VIEWS: Use random sampling with smart sample sizes
        -- =====================================================================
        ELSE
            format(
                $187 || $188 ||
                $189 || $190 ||
                $191 || $192 || $193 || $194 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $195 THEN $196 || $197
                    ELSE $198 || $199
                END,
                extraction_type,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                row_count,
                schemaname,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix,
                file_prefix
            )
    END AS extraction_command
FROM view_extract
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $200 THEN $201
        WHEN $202 THEN $203
        WHEN $204 THEN $205
        WHEN $206 THEN $207
        WHEN $208 THEN $209
        ELSE $210
    END,
    viewname

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Get all materialized view dependencies
view_deps AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        dependent_view.relname AS view_name,
        source_table.relname AS depends_on,
        CASE 
            WHEN source_table.relkind = $1 THEN $2
            ELSE $3
        END AS depends_on_matview
    FROM pg_depend
    JOIN pg_rewrite ON pg_depend.objid = pg_rewrite.oid
    JOIN pg_class AS dependent_view ON pg_rewrite.ev_class = dependent_view.oid
    JOIN pg_class AS source_table ON pg_depend.refobjid = source_table.oid
    JOIN pg_namespace dependent_ns ON dependent_ns.oid = dependent_view.relnamespace
    JOIN pg_namespace source_ns ON source_ns.oid = source_table.relnamespace
    WHERE dependent_view.relkind = $4
      AND source_table.relkind IN ($5, $6, $7)
      AND pg_depend.deptype = $8
      AND dependent_ns.nspname = $9
      AND source_ns.nspname = $10
),
-- Build dependency graph (only MV dependencies)
mv_dependencies AS (
    SELECT view_name, depends_on
    FROM view_deps
    WHERE depends_on_matview = $11
),
-- Get all materialized views
all_mvs AS (
    SELECT matviewname AS view_name
    FROM pg_matviews
    WHERE schemaname = $12
),
-- Calculate dependency depth using recursive CTE
dependency_depth AS (
    -- Base case: views with no MV dependencies
    SELECT 
        am.view_name,
        $13 AS depth,
        ARRAY[am.view_name] AS path
    FROM all_mvs am
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT $14 FROM mv_dependencies md 
        WHERE md.view_name = am.view_name
    )
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: views that depend on views we've already processed
    SELECT 
        md.view_name,
        dd.depth + $15,
        dd.path || md.view_name
    FROM mv_dependencies md
    JOIN dependency_depth dd ON md.depends_on = dd.view_name
    WHERE NOT (md.view_name = ANY(dd.path)) -- Prevent cycles
),
-- Get maximum depth for each view
max_depths AS (
    SELECT 
        view_name,
        MAX(depth) as max_depth
    FROM dependency_depth
    GROUP BY view_name
)
-- Output in refresh order
SELECT 
    max_depth AS dependency_level,
    view_name,
    COALESCE(
        (SELECT string_agg(depends_on, $16 ORDER BY depends_on)
         FROM mv_dependencies md
         WHERE md.view_name = md2.view_name),
        $17
    ) AS depends_on_matviews,
    format($18, $19, view_name) AS refresh_command
FROM max_depths md2
ORDER BY max_depth, view_name

SELECT
    $1 AS category,
    $2 || m.matviewname AS check_name,
    CASE
        WHEN s.last_vacuum IS NULL AND s.last_autovacuum IS NULL THEN $3
        WHEN GREATEST(s.last_vacuum, s.last_autovacuum) < NOW() - INTERVAL $4 THEN $5
        WHEN m.ispopulated = $6 THEN $7

    CASE
        WHEN m.ispopulated = $9 THEN $10
        WHEN s.last_vacuum IS NULL AND s.last_autovacuum IS NULL THEN $11
        WHEN GREATEST(s.last_vacuum, s.last_autovacuum) < NOW() - INTERVAL $12 THEN $13
        ELSE $14
    END AS severity,
    CASE
        WHEN m.ispopulated = $15 THEN $16
        WHEN s.last_vacuum IS NULL AND s.last_autovacuum IS NULL THEN $17
        ELSE $18 || COALESCE(GREATEST(s.last_vacuum, s.last_autovacuum)::TEXT, $19)
    END AS details,
    CASE
        WHEN m.ispopulated = $20 OR (s.last_vacuum IS NULL AND s.last_autovacuum IS NULL)
        THEN $21
        WHEN GREATEST(s.last_vacuum, s.last_autovacuum) < NOW() - INTERVAL $22
        THEN $23 || m.matviewname || $24
        ELSE $25
    END AS recommendation
FROM pg_matviews m
LEFT JOIN pg_stat_user_tables s ON s.schemaname = m.schemaname AND s.relname = m.matviewname
WHERE m.schemaname = $26

WITH table_counts AS (
    SELECT t.schemaname,
           t.tablename,
           COALESCE(c.reltuples, $1)::BIGINT AS row_count
    FROM pg_tables t
    JOIN pg_class c ON c.relname = t.tablename
    JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace AND n.nspname = t.schemaname
    WHERE t.schemaname = $2
),
table_extract AS (
    SELECT schemaname,
           tablename,
           row_count,
           -- Smart sample size calculation:
           -- 1. Complete extraction for small tables (< threshold)
           -- 2. Extended samples for party/committee/person data
           -- 3. Extended samples for document/voting/worldbank data
           -- 4. Default sample size for others
           CASE 
               -- Complete extraction for small tables
               WHEN row_count <= $3::int THEN row_count
               -- Extended samples for party-related tables
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $4 OR tablename ILIKE $5 THEN 
                   LEAST($6::int, row_count)
               -- Extended samples for committee-related tables
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $7 OR tablename ILIKE $8 THEN 
                   LEAST($9::int, row_count)
               -- Extended samples for person/politician data
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $10 OR tablename ILIKE $11 
                   OR tablename ILIKE $12 THEN 
                   LEAST($13::int, row_count)
               -- Extended samples for document-related tables
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $14 OR tablename ILIKE $15 THEN 
                   LEAST($16::int, row_count)
               -- Extended samples for voting-related tables
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $17 OR tablename ILIKE $18 THEN 
                   LEAST($19::int, row_count)
               -- Extended samples for worldbank data
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $20 OR tablename ILIKE $21 
                   OR tablename ILIKE $22 THEN 
                   LEAST($23::int, row_count)
               -- Default sample size
               ELSE LEAST($24::int, row_count)
           END AS sample_rows,
           -- Extraction type for logging
           CASE 
               WHEN row_count <= $25::int THEN $26
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $27 OR tablename ILIKE $28 THEN $29
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $30 OR tablename ILIKE $31 THEN $32
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $33 OR tablename ILIKE $34 
                   OR tablename ILIKE $35 THEN $36
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $37 OR tablename ILIKE $38 THEN $39
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $40 OR tablename ILIKE $41 THEN $42
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $43 OR tablename ILIKE $44 
                   OR tablename ILIKE $45 THEN $46
               ELSE $47
           END AS extraction_type,
           CASE WHEN tablename LIKE $48 THEN tablename ELSE $49 || tablename END AS file_prefix
    FROM table_counts
    -- CHANGED: Include ALL tables, even empty ones (for header-only CSV files)
    -- WHERE row_count > 0  -- REMOVED: No longer skip empty tables
)
SELECT format(
    $50 || $51 ||
    $52 || $53 ||
    $54 || $55 || $56 || $57 ||
    CASE 
        WHEN row_count = $58 THEN $59 || $60
        ELSE $61 || $62
    END,
    extraction_type,
    schemaname,
    tablename,
    sample_rows,
    row_count,
    schemaname,
    tablename,
    sample_rows,
    file_prefix,
    tablename,
    row_count,
    row_count,
    file_prefix,
    file_prefix
)
FROM table_extract
ORDER BY tablename

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_view_levels AS
WITH RECURSIVE 
all_views AS (
    SELECT viewname AS view_name, $1 AS view_type
    FROM pg_views 
    WHERE schemaname = $2
      AND viewname NOT LIKE $3
    UNION ALL
    SELECT matviewname AS view_name, $4 AS view_type
    FROM pg_matviews 
    WHERE schemaname = $5
      AND matviewname NOT LIKE $6
),
view_only_deps AS (
    SELECT vd.view_name, vd.depends_on
    FROM tmp_view_dependencies vd
    WHERE vd.dependency_type IN ($7, $8)
),
dependency_depth AS (
    -- Base case: views with no view dependencies (only table dependencies)
    SELECT 
        av.view_name,
        av.view_type,
        $9 AS depth,
        ARRAY[av.view_name] AS path
    FROM all_views av
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT $10 FROM view_only_deps vod 
        WHERE vod.view_name = av.view_name
    )
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: views depending on already processed views
    SELECT 
        vod.view_name,
        av.view_type,
        dd.depth + $11,
        dd.path || vod.view_name
    FROM view_only_deps vod
    JOIN dependency_depth dd ON vod.depends_on = dd.view_name
    JOIN all_views av ON vod.view_name = av.view_name
    WHERE NOT (vod.view_name = ANY(dd.path)) -- Prevent cycles
)
SELECT 
    view_name,
    view_type,
    MAX(depth) as dependency_level,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(depth) BETWEEN $12 AND $13 THEN $14
        WHEN MAX(depth) = $15 THEN $16
        WHEN MAX(depth) = $17 THEN $18
        WHEN MAX(depth) >= $19 THEN $20
    END AS complexity_category
FROM dependency_depth
GROUP BY view_name, view_type

DO $$
DECLARE
    v_null_pct NUMERIC;
    v_critical_columns TEXT[][] := ARRAY[
        ['person_data', 'first_name'],
        ['person_data', 'last_name'],
        ['assignment_data', 'org_code'],
        ['vote_data', 'vote']
    ];
    v_col TEXT[];
BEGIN
    FOREACH v_col SLICE 1 IN ARRAY v_critical_columns
    LOOP
        BEGIN
            EXECUTE format(
                'SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE %I IS NULL) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 2) FROM %I',
                v_col[2], v_col[1]
            ) INTO v_null_pct;
            
            INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
                'Data Quality',
                'NULL Percentage: ' || v_col[1] || '.' || v_col[2],
                CASE 
                    WHEN v_null_pct > 10 THEN 'FAIL'
                    WHEN v_null_pct > 5 THEN 'WARN'
                    ELSE 'PASS'
                END,
                CASE 
                    WHEN v_null_pct > 10 THEN 3
                    WHEN v_null_pct > 5 THEN 2
                    ELSE 1
                END,
                COALESCE(v_null_pct::TEXT, '0') || '% NULL values',
                CASE 
                    WHEN v_null_pct > 5 THEN 'Investigate and fix NULL values in ' || v_col[1] || '.' || v_col[2]
                    ELSE NULL
                END
            );
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            -- Table or column doesn't exist, skip
            NULL;
        END;
    END LOOP;
END $$

DO $$
BEGIN
    IF EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_extension WHERE extname = 'pg_stat_statements') THEN
        INSERT INTO health_check_results
        SELECT
            'Performance' AS category,
            'Slow Query: ' || LEFT(query, 50) || '...' AS check_name,
            'WARN' AS status,
            2 AS severity,
            'Avg time: ' || ROUND(mean_exec_time::NUMERIC, 2) || 'ms | Calls: ' || calls AS details,
            'Optimize query or add indexes' AS recommendation
        FROM pg_stat_statements
        WHERE mean_exec_time > 1000  -- > 1 second
        ORDER BY mean_exec_time DESC
        LIMIT 10;
    ELSE
        INSERT INTO health_check_results VALUES (
            'Performance',
            'pg_stat_statements Extension',
            'INFO',
            1,
            'Extension not enabled',
            'Enable pg_stat_statements for query performance tracking: CREATE EXTENSION pg_stat_statements;'
        );
    END IF;
END $$

WITH RECURSIVE 
all_views AS (
    SELECT viewname AS view_name, $1 AS view_type
    FROM pg_views 
    WHERE schemaname = $2
      AND viewname NOT LIKE $3
    UNION ALL
    SELECT matviewname AS view_name, $4 AS view_type
    FROM pg_matviews 
    WHERE schemaname = $5
      AND matviewname NOT LIKE $6
),
view_deps AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        dependent_view.relname AS view_name,
        source_table.relname AS depends_on,
        CASE source_table.relkind
            WHEN $7 THEN $8
            WHEN $9 THEN $10
            WHEN $11 THEN $12
        END AS dependency_type
    FROM pg_depend
    JOIN pg_rewrite ON pg_depend.objid = pg_rewrite.oid
    JOIN pg_class AS dependent_view ON pg_rewrite.ev_class = dependent_view.oid
    JOIN pg_class AS source_table ON pg_depend.refobjid = source_table.oid
    JOIN pg_namespace dependent_ns ON dependent_ns.oid = dependent_view.relnamespace
    JOIN pg_namespace source_ns ON source_ns.oid = source_table.relnamespace
    WHERE dependent_view.relkind IN ($13, $14)
      AND source_table.relkind IN ($15, $16, $17)
      AND pg_depend.deptype = $18
      AND dependent_ns.nspname = $19
      AND source_ns.nspname = $20
),
view_only_deps AS (
    SELECT view_name, depends_on
    FROM view_deps
    WHERE dependency_type IN ($21, $22)
),
dependency_depth AS (
    SELECT av.view_name, av.view_type, $23 AS depth
    FROM all_views av
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT $24 FROM view_only_deps vod WHERE vod.view_name = av.view_name)
    UNION ALL
    SELECT vod.view_name, av.view_type, dd.depth + $25
    FROM view_only_deps vod
    JOIN dependency_depth dd ON vod.depends_on = dd.view_name
    JOIN all_views av ON vod.view_name = av.view_name
),
max_depths AS (
    SELECT view_name, view_type, MAX(depth) as max_depth
    FROM dependency_depth
    GROUP BY view_name, view_type
)
SELECT 
    $26 AS metric,
    COUNT(*)::TEXT AS value
FROM all_views
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $27,
    COUNT(*)::TEXT
FROM all_views WHERE view_type = $28
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $29,
    COUNT(*)::TEXT
FROM all_views WHERE view_type = $30
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $31,
    COUNT(*)::TEXT
FROM max_depths WHERE max_depth <= $32
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $33,
    COUNT(*)::TEXT
FROM max_depths WHERE max_depth = $34
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $35,
    COUNT(*)::TEXT
FROM max_depths WHERE max_depth = $36
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $37,
    COUNT(*)::TEXT
FROM max_depths WHERE max_depth >= $38

SELECT 
    temporal_granularity AS "Granularity",
    COUNT(*) AS "View Count",
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $1 THEN $2
        WHEN $3 THEN $4
        WHEN $5 THEN $6
        WHEN $7 THEN $8
        WHEN $9 THEN $10
        ELSE $11
    END AS "Sampling Strategy"
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $12::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $13
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $14::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $15
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
) view_classification
GROUP BY temporal_granularity
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $16 THEN $17
        WHEN $18 THEN $19
        WHEN $20 THEN $21
        WHEN $22 THEN $23
        WHEN $24 THEN $25
        ELSE $26
    END

WITH view_deps AS (
        SELECT 
            dependent_view.relname AS view_name,
            COUNT(DISTINCT source_table.relname) AS dep_count
        FROM pg_depend
        JOIN pg_rewrite ON pg_depend.objid = pg_rewrite.oid
        JOIN pg_class AS dependent_view ON pg_rewrite.ev_class = dependent_view.oid
        JOIN pg_class AS source_table ON pg_depend.refobjid = source_table.oid
        WHERE dependent_view.relkind IN ($1, $2)
            AND source_table.relkind IN ($3, $4, $5)
            AND dependent_view.relnamespace = (SELECT oid FROM pg_namespace WHERE nspname = $6)
        GROUP BY dependent_view.relname
    )
    SELECT 
        COALESCE(MAX(dep_count), $7),
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE dep_count > $8),
        COUNT(*)
                                               FROM view_deps

WITH view_counts AS (
    SELECT schemaname,
           viewname,
           row_count
    FROM cia_view_row_counts
    WHERE viewname != $1
      AND viewname != $2
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL VIEW CLASSIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
-- Identify views by temporal granularity based on naming patterns
-- This enables stratified sampling across time periods
-- Uses reusable cia_classify_temporal_view() function
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        vc.schemaname,
        vc.viewname,
        vc.row_count,
        c.temporal_granularity,
        c.samples_per_bucket
    FROM view_counts vc
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(vc.viewname) AS c
    WHERE vc.row_count > $3
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL COLUMN DETECTION
-- ============================================================================
-- Automatically detect temporal columns in each view
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_columns AS (
    SELECT 
        c.table_schema AS schemaname,
        c.table_name AS viewname,
        c.column_name AS temporal_column,
        c.data_type,
        -- Prioritize common temporal column names
        CASE 
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($4, $5) THEN $6
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($7, $8, $9) THEN $10
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($11, $12, $13) THEN $14
            WHEN c.column_name LIKE $15 THEN $16
            ELSE $17
        END AS priority
    FROM information_schema.columns c
    WHERE c.table_schema = $18
      AND c.data_type IN ($19, $20, $21)
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($22, $23) -- Exclude technical columns
),
view_primary_temporal_column AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (schemaname, viewname)
        schemaname,
        viewname,
        temporal_column
    FROM view_temporal_columns
    ORDER BY schemaname, viewname, priority
),
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE STRATIFIED SAMPLING QUERIES
-- ============================================================================
view_extract AS (
    SELECT 
        vtc.schemaname,
        vtc.viewname,
        vtc.row_count,
        vtc.temporal_granularity,
        vtc.samples_per_bucket,
        vptc.temporal_column,
        -- Smart sample size calculation:
        -- 1. Complete extraction for small views (< threshold)
        -- 2. Extended samples for analytical trend views (election proximity, seasonal activity, career trajectory)
        -- 3. Extended samples for party/committee/person views
        -- 4. Extended samples for document/voting/worldbank views
        -- 5. Default sample size for others
        CASE 
            -- Complete extraction for small views
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $24::int THEN vtc.row_count
            -- Extended samples for analytical trend views (500 rows for better temporal coverage)
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($25,
                                   $26,
                                   $27) THEN
                LEAST($28::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for party-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $29 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $30 THEN 
                LEAST($31::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for committee-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $32 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $33 THEN 
                LEAST($34::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for person/politician views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $35 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $36 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $37 THEN 
                LEAST($38::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for document-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $39 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $40 THEN 
                LEAST($41::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for voting-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $42 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $43 THEN 
                LEAST($44::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for worldbank data
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $45 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $46 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $47 THEN 
                LEAST($48::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Default sample size
            ELSE LEAST($49::int, vtc.row_count)
        END AS sample_rows,
        -- Extraction type for logging
        CASE 
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $50::int THEN $51
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($52,
                                   $53,
                                   $54) THEN $55
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $56 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $57 THEN $58
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $59 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $60 THEN $61
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $62 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $63 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $64 THEN $65
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $66 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $67 THEN $68
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $69 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $70 THEN $71
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $72 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $73 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $74 THEN $75
            ELSE $76
        END AS extraction_type,
        CASE WHEN vtc.viewname LIKE $77 THEN vtc.viewname ELSE $78 || vtc.viewname END AS file_prefix
    FROM view_temporal_classification vtc
    LEFT JOIN view_primary_temporal_column vptc 
        ON vtc.schemaname = vptc.schemaname 
        AND vtc.viewname = vptc.viewname
)
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE EXTRACTION COMMANDS WITH TEMPORAL STRATIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
SELECT 
    CASE 
        -- =====================================================================
        -- DAILY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by day
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $79 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $80 || $81 ||
                $82 ||
                $83 ||
                $84 ||
                $85 ||
                $86 ||
                $87 ||
                $88 ||
                $89 ||
                $90 || $91 ||
                $92 || $93,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- WEEKLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by week
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $94 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $95 || $96 ||
                $97 ||
                $98 ||
                $99 ||
                $100 ||
                $101 ||
                $102 ||
                $103 ||
                $104 ||
                $105 || $106 ||
                $107 || $108,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- MONTHLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by month
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $109 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $110 || $111 ||
                $112 ||
                $113 ||
                $114 ||
                $115 ||
                $116 ||
                $117 ||
                $118 ||
                $119 ||
                $120 || $121 ||
                $122 || $123,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- ANNUAL VIEWS: Stratified sampling by year
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $124 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $125 || $126 ||
                $127 ||
                $128 ||
                $129 ||
                $130 ||
                $131 ||
                $132 ||
                $133 ||
                $134 || $135 ||
                $136 || $137,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- TEMPORAL TREND VIEWS: Sample across all time periods
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $138 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $139 || $140 ||
                $141 ||
                $142 ||
                $143 ||
                $144 ||
                $145 ||
                $146 ||
                $147 ||
                $148 ||
                $149 || $150 ||
                $151 || $152,
                viewname,
                temporal_column, temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- NON-TEMPORAL VIEWS: Use random sampling with smart sample sizes
        -- =====================================================================
        ELSE
            format(
                $153 || $154 ||
                $155 || $156 ||
                $157 || $158,
                extraction_type,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                row_count,
                schemaname,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                file_prefix
            )
    END AS extraction_command
FROM view_extract
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $159 THEN $160
        WHEN $161 THEN $162
        WHEN $163 THEN $164
        WHEN $165 THEN $166
        WHEN $167 THEN $168
        ELSE $169
    END,
    viewname

WITH table_counts AS (
    SELECT t.schemaname,
           t.tablename,
           COALESCE(c.reltuples, $1)::BIGINT AS row_count
    FROM pg_tables t
    JOIN pg_class c ON c.relname = t.tablename
    JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace AND n.nspname = t.schemaname
    WHERE t.schemaname = $2
),
table_extract AS (
    SELECT schemaname,
           tablename,
           row_count,
           -- Smart sample size calculation:
           -- 1. Complete extraction for small tables (< threshold)
           -- 2. Extended samples for party/committee/person data
           -- 3. Extended samples for document/voting/worldbank data
           -- 4. Default sample size for others
           CASE 
               -- Complete extraction for small tables
               WHEN row_count <= $3::int THEN row_count
               -- Extended samples for party-related tables
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $4 OR tablename ILIKE $5 THEN 
                   LEAST($6::int, row_count)
               -- Extended samples for committee-related tables
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $7 OR tablename ILIKE $8 THEN 
                   LEAST($9::int, row_count)
               -- Extended samples for person/politician data
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $10 OR tablename ILIKE $11 
                   OR tablename ILIKE $12 THEN 
                   LEAST($13::int, row_count)
               -- Extended samples for document-related tables
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $14 OR tablename ILIKE $15 THEN 
                   LEAST($16::int, row_count)
               -- Extended samples for voting-related tables
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $17 OR tablename ILIKE $18 THEN 
                   LEAST($19::int, row_count)
               -- Extended samples for worldbank data
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $20 OR tablename ILIKE $21 
                   OR tablename ILIKE $22 THEN 
                   LEAST($23::int, row_count)
               -- Default sample size
               ELSE LEAST($24::int, row_count)
           END AS sample_rows,
           -- Extraction type for logging
           CASE 
               WHEN row_count <= $25::int THEN $26
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $27 OR tablename ILIKE $28 THEN $29
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $30 OR tablename ILIKE $31 THEN $32
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $33 OR tablename ILIKE $34 
                   OR tablename ILIKE $35 THEN $36
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $37 OR tablename ILIKE $38 THEN $39
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $40 OR tablename ILIKE $41 THEN $42
               WHEN tablename ILIKE $43 OR tablename ILIKE $44 
                   OR tablename ILIKE $45 THEN $46
               ELSE $47
           END AS extraction_type,
           CASE WHEN tablename LIKE $48 THEN tablename ELSE $49 || tablename END AS file_prefix
    FROM table_counts
    WHERE row_count > $50
)
SELECT format(
    $51 || $52 ||
    $53 || $54 ||
    $55 || $56,
    extraction_type,
    schemaname,
    tablename,
    sample_rows,
    row_count,
    schemaname,
    tablename,
    sample_rows,
    file_prefix,
    file_prefix
)
FROM table_extract
ORDER BY tablename

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_view_levels AS
WITH RECURSIVE 
all_views AS (
    SELECT viewname AS view_name, $1 AS view_type
    FROM pg_views 
    WHERE schemaname = $2
      AND viewname NOT LIKE $3
    UNION ALL
    SELECT matviewname AS view_name, $4 AS view_type
    FROM pg_matviews 
    WHERE schemaname = $5
      AND matviewname NOT LIKE $6
),
view_only_deps AS (
    SELECT vd.view_name, vd.depends_on
    FROM tmp_view_dependencies vd
    WHERE vd.dependency_type IN ($7, $8)
),
dependency_depth AS (
    -- Base case: views with no view dependencies (only table dependencies)
    SELECT 
        av.view_name,
        av.view_type,
        $9 AS depth,
        ARRAY[av.view_name] AS path
    FROM all_views av
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT $10 FROM view_only_deps vod 
        WHERE vod.view_name = av.view_name
    )
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: views depending on already processed views
    SELECT 
        vod.view_name,
        av.view_type,
        dd.depth + $11,
        dd.path || vod.view_name
    FROM view_only_deps vod
    JOIN dependency_depth dd ON vod.depends_on = dd.view_name
    JOIN all_views av ON vod.view_name = av.view_name
    WHERE NOT (vod.view_name = ANY(dd.path)) -- Prevent cycles
)
SELECT 
    view_name,
    view_type,
    MAX(depth) as dependency_level,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(depth) BETWEEN $12 AND $13 THEN $14
        WHEN MAX(depth) = $15 THEN $16
        WHEN MAX(depth) = $17 THEN $18
        WHEN MAX(depth) >= $19 THEN $20
    END AS complexity_category
FROM dependency_depth
GROUP BY view_name, view_type
